expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: search box selector dropdown planning covers OK or Cancel confirmation, pending selection, and dismissal outcomes
expectations.evidence: The Phase 4a output explicitly covers all required elements: it defines "Committed selection" vs "Pending selection" in the state model, and includes dedicated sections for "2.1 OK / Confirm behavior", "2.2 Cancel behavior", and "4. Dismissal outcomes (non-explicit close)" with an explicit requirement to define whether dismissal behaves like Cancel or Confirm. It also includes a "Test coverage checklist" with OK, Cancel, and dismissal paths.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The document is titled "QA Plan Orchestrator — Phase 4a Output (Advisory)" and repeatedly frames content as "Phase 4a — Confirmation / Commit Semantics" with "Objective (Phase 4a contract focus)" and phase4a-specific deliverable requirements (e.g., "Phase 4a requires a defined policy for closing without OK/Cancel"). The content is focused on commit/confirmation semantics, which matches phase4a.
